mission.title: external-secrets: Support google workload identity federation
mission.description: Support google workload identity federation. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
Support for workload identity federation using credential configuration files.

Currently, if the credential configuration files is specified as secretAccessKeySecretRef, the following error will occur.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support google workload identity federation
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

The GCPSM documentation was missing the description of the (arguably) simplest WIF approach, and in my experience the order made it difficult for newcomers to understand the available authentication options and implications.
N/A
I revised the authentication section and the
```yaml
failed to create GCP secretmanager client: failed to process the provided JSON credentials: google: read JWT from JSON credentials: 'type' field is "external_account" (expected "service_account")
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support google workload identity federation" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

The GCPSM documentation was missing the description of the (arguably) simplest WIF approach, and in my experience the order made it difficult for newcomers to understand the available authentication options and implications.
codeSnippets: failed to create GCP secretmanager client: failed to process the provided JSON credentials: google: read JWT from JSON credentials: 'type' field is "external_account" (expected "service_account")

apiVersion: generators.external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: GCRAccessToken
metadata:
  name: gcr-gen
spec:
  projectID: "my-project"
  auth:
    jwt:
        workloadIdentityPool: foo
        provider: bar
        serviceAccountRef:
	    name: service-account-token-to-use
            audience:
	        - foo
	        - bar

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
